title: calophyllum nubicola
intro: Calophyllum nubicola, also known as the Mountain Calophyllum or Mountain Pukul, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the Calophyllaceae family and is native to the tropical regions of Malaysia and Indonesia. The medicinal properties of Calophyllum nubicola lie in its seeds, which contain a rich source of calophyllolide, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The herbal preparations of Calophyllum nubicola involve extracting the calophyllolide from the seeds, which can be used to create topical creams, ointments, and oils for external application.
